title: Distill a causal multi-task sequence model into the tabular ensemble
summary: Recent pit-stop papers report that sequence models help because they capture temporal dependencies, and SOSTA explicitly couples next-lap classification with stop-horizon regression.
rationale: Recent pit-stop papers report that sequence models help because they capture temporal dependencies, and SOSTA explicitly couples next-lap classification with stop-horizon regression. Instead of replacing the strong tabular stack, use a small causal sequence model as a teacher and feed its OOF signals into XGB/Cat.
implementation_hint: Build short causal windows per driver-race-stint, e.g. last 8-12 laps, with only past information. Train a compact GRU/BiGRU-free causal model with two heads: `PitNextLap` and `laps_to_next_pit`. Generate leakage-safe OOF predictions for both heads and add them as numeric features to the existing tabular models or to a final stacked blender.
expected_effect: Useful if the current tree ensemble misses temporal acceleration patterns before stops; the regression head should regularize the classifier and produce a more informative teacher than a single binary target.
risk: Sequence preprocessing is more brittle than trees; if windows are not strictly causal or folds are not group-safe, leakage will swamp the result.
